description: Port chenglou/pretext to Lynx — pure JS text measurement & layout using main-thread lynx.getTextInfo() as measurement primitive. Reference source repo: ../pretext (~/github/pretext). Copy and adapt src/ files from there.

userStories.title: Rspeedy project scaffolding
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a working Rspeedy project skeleton with TypeScript config, @formatjs/intl-segmenter dependency, and a hello-world entry point that renders on Lynx.
acceptanceCriteria: Rspeedy project initialized with TypeScript config and src/ + pages/ directories
@formatjs/intl-segmenter installed as dependency
A hello-world page entry exists under pages/ and renders a <text> element
rspeedy dev launches without errors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the foundation. All subsequent stories depend on this. Use ReactLynx for pages, library code is plain TS under src/.

userStories.title: Analysis types and whitespace normalization
userStories.description: As the library, I need the core types (SegmentBreakKind, TextAnalysis, etc.) and whitespace normalization functions ported from Pretext's analysis.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: src/analysis.ts exists with exported types: WhiteSpaceMode, SegmentBreakKind ('text'|'space'|'preserved-space'|'tab'|'glue'|'zero-width-break'|'soft-hyphen'|'hard-break'), MergedSegmentation, AnalysisChunk, TextAnalysis, AnalysisProfile
normalizeWhitespaceNormal() collapses runs of whitespace to single space, trims leading/trailing spaces

normalizeWhitespacePreWrap() normalizes 
 to 
 and /  to 
, preserves spaces and tabs
classifySegmentBreakChar() correctly classifies NBSP as 'glue', ZWSP as 'zero-width-break', soft-hyphen as 'soft-hyphen', etc.
splitSegmentByBreakKind() splits a segment string into pieces by break kind
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Source: ../pretext/src/analysis.ts (lines 1-385). Port the top half: types, whitespace normalization, and segment classification. Does NOT include the merge pipeline or analyzeText() yet.

userStories.title: Analysis character sets and merge helpers
userStories.description: As the library, I need the CJK detection, kinsoku sets, punctuation sets, and merge helper functions from analysis.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: isCJK() detects CJK unified ideographs, kana, hangul, fullwidth chars across BMP and supplementary planes
kinsokuStart set contains fullwidth comma/period/brackets/prolonged sound mark/iteration marks
kinsokuEnd set contains opening brackets and quotes
leftStickyPunctuation set contains period, comma, closing brackets, percentage, closing quotes, ellipsis, Arabic/Devanagari/Myanmar punctuation
isLeftStickyPunctuationSegment(), isForwardStickyClusterSegment(), isEscapedQuoteClusterSegment() work correctly
splitTrailingForwardStickyCluster() splits trailing opening brackets/quotes from a CJK segment
endsWithClosingQuote() detects trailing closing quotes including CJK brackets
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Source: ../pretext/src/analysis.ts (lines 97-320). Port isCJK, kinsoku sets, punctuation sets, and merge helper predicates. These are used by the merge pipeline in the next story.

userStories.title: Analysis merge pipeline and analyzeText
userStories.description: As the library, I need the full segment merge pipeline (punctuation merging, URL merging, numeric runs, glue runs, CJK kinsoku) and the top-level analyzeText() function.
acceptanceCriteria: buildMergedSegmentation() uses Intl.Segmenter polyfill for word segmentation and applies all merge rules: left-sticky punctuation, forward-sticky clusters, escaped quote clusters, CJK kinsoku line-start prohibition, Arabic no-space punctuation, Myanmar medial glue, repeated single-char runs
mergeUrlLikeRuns() merges URL-like segments (http://... and www.) up to query boundary
mergeUrlQueryRuns() merges query string segments after the URL path
mergeNumericRuns() merges digit+joiner sequences like '7:00-9:00'
mergeGlueConnectedTextRuns() merges NBSP-connected text into single non-breakable units
compileAnalysisChunks() splits segments at hard-break boundaries for pre-wrap mode
analyzeText('hello world', profile) returns TextAnalysis with 3 segments: 'hello', ' ', 'world'
analyzeText works with pre-wrap mode preserving hard breaks
setAnalysisLocale() and clearAnalysisCaches() exported and functional
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Source: ../pretext/src/analysis.ts (lines 320-1008). Port buildMergedSegmentation, all merge* functions, compileAnalysisChunks, analyzeText. Replace native Intl.Segmenter with @formatjs/intl-segmenter polyfill.

userStories.title: Text measurement via getTextInfo
userStories.description: As the library, I need to measure segment widths using Lynx's main-thread lynx.getTextInfo() API, replacing Pretext's Canvas measureText.
acceptanceCriteria: src/measurement.ts exists with getSegmentMetrics() that calls lynx.getTextInfo(seg, { fontSize, fontFamily }) and returns { width, containsCJK }
parseFontSize() extracts px value from font strings like '16px Inter' or '700 20px Palatino'
parseFontFamily() extracts font family name from font shorthand string
Segment metrics cache: Map<font, Map<segment, SegmentMetrics>> via getSegmentMetricCache()
getCorrectedSegmentWidth() returns metrics.width (emoji correction = 0 for MVP)
getSegmentGraphemeWidths() splits a segment into graphemes via polyfill and measures each
getSegmentGraphemePrefixWidths() measures cumulative prefix widths for Safari-style breakable runs
getFontMeasurementState(font, needsEmoji) returns { cache, fontSize, emojiCorrection }

getEngineProfile() returns a single Lynx profile (no UA sniffing): { lineFitEpsilon: 0.005, carryCJKAfterClosingQuote: false, preferPrefixWidthsForBreakableRuns: false, preferEarlySoftHyphenBreak: false }
clearMeasurementCaches() clears all caches
textMayContainEmoji() regex check exported
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Source: ../pretext/src/measurement.ts. Key adaptation: replace getMeasureContext()/OffscreenCanvas with lynx.getTextInfo(). Add parseFontFamily(). Emoji correction set to 0 for MVP. getEngineProfile() returns single Lynx profile instead of UA-sniffing.

userStories.title: Line breaking engine
userStories.description: As the library, I need the pure-arithmetic line breaking engine that walks cached segment widths to count lines and compute positions.
acceptanceCriteria: src/line-break.ts ported 1:1 from Pretext (zero platform dependencies in this file)
countPreparedLines() returns correct line count for simple text
countPreparedLinesSimple() fast path handles text + space + zero-width-break only
walkPreparedLines() calls back with InternalLayoutLine for each line including start/end segment indices and width
layoutNextLineRange() returns one line at a time for streaming layout, or null when exhausted
normalizeLineStart() skips leading spaces/ZWSP/soft-hyphens at line starts
Full path handles soft-hyphen (discretionary hyphen width added at break), tab (tab-stop advance), hard-break, preserved-space
Trailing whitespace hangs past maxWidth without triggering line break
Words wider than maxWidth break at grapheme boundaries using breakableWidths
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Source: ../pretext/src/line-break.ts. Direct copy with only import path changes — zero platform dependencies.

userStories.title: Public API — prepare and layout
userStories.description: As the library, I need the core prepare() + layout() public API that ties analysis, measurement, and line breaking together.
acceptanceCriteria: src/layout.ts exports prepare(text, font, options?) returning opaque PreparedText
src/layout.ts exports layout(prepared, maxWidth, lineHeight) returning { lineCount, height }
prepare() internally calls analyzeText() then measureAnalysis() which measures each segment via getTextInfo
CJK segments are split into graphemes with kinsoku merging during measurement
Soft-hyphen, hard-break, and tab segments get correct zero/special widths
Word-like segments longer than 1 char get pre-measured grapheme widths for overflow-wrap
layout() calls countPreparedLines() — zero native calls, pure arithmetic
clearCache() resets analysis + measurement + grapheme caches
setLocale(locale?) retargets the word segmenter and clears caches
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Source: ../pretext/src/layout.ts (types, measureAnalysis, prepareInternal, prepare, layout, clearCache, setLocale). Port PreparedCore type, measureAnalysis(), and the prepare/layout public API.

userStories.title: Public API — rich layout functions
userStories.description: As the library, I need prepareWithSegments, layoutWithLines, walkLineRanges, and layoutNextLine for manual line-level layout.
acceptanceCriteria: prepareWithSegments(text, font, options?) returns PreparedTextWithSegments with .segments string array
layoutWithLines(prepared, maxWidth, lineHeight) returns { lineCount, height, lines: LayoutLine[] }
Each LayoutLine has { text, width, start: LayoutCursor, end: LayoutCursor }
walkLineRanges(prepared, maxWidth, onLine) calls back with LayoutLineRange (width + cursors, no text materialization)
layoutNextLine(prepared, start, maxWidth) returns LayoutLine or null for streaming variable-width layout
Soft-hyphen lines include visible trailing '-' in line.text
All types exported: PreparedText, PreparedTextWithSegments, LayoutCursor, LayoutResult, LayoutLine, LayoutLineRange, LayoutLinesResult, PrepareOptions
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Source: ../pretext/src/layout.ts (rich-path: buildLineTextFromRange, materializeLayoutLine, toLayoutLineRange, walkLineRanges, layoutNextLine, layoutWithLines) + ../pretext/src/bidi.ts (stub computeSegmentLevels to return null for MVP).

userStories.title: Line-by-line accuracy validation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify that lynx-pretext's line breaking matches getTextInfo's native line breaking, by comparing per-line text content.
acceptanceCriteria: Page entry at pages/accuracy.tsx renders in Lynx
Corpus of ~20 diverse texts: English, CJK, mixed scripts, emoji, long words, short single-line text

For each text at 10 widths (50px to 500px in 50px steps): call getTextInfo(text, { fontSize, fontFamily, maxWidth }) to get native content[] and layoutWithLines(prepared, width, lineHeight) to get our lines[].text
Line-by-line comparison: native.content[i].trim() === lines[i].text.trim() for all i
Also compare line count: native.content.length === lines.length
Also compare total width: getTextInfo(text, { fontSize, fontFamily }).width vs sum-of-segment-widths (single-line case)
Display per-text pass/fail with first divergent line highlighted on mismatch
Overall pass rate displayed as percentage
Target: > 90% match rate for English text, document all mismatches
If mismatches found: log the text, width, expected line content, actual line content for debugging
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: CRITICAL: This is the verification gate. Run BEFORE building demos. The oracle is getTextInfo(fullText, { maxWidth }) which does its own native line breaking and returns content[]. Compare against layoutWithLines().lines[].text. Mismatches indicate either: (1) segment widths don't sum correctly (kerning/shaping), (2) line-break logic differs, or (3) analysis produced wrong segments. Each mismatch type has a different fix path. Start with lineFitEpsilon=0.005 and tune if needed.

userStories.title: Demo — basic height measurement
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Lynx page that measures a paragraph's height using prepare() + layout() and compares it to native <text> height.
acceptanceCriteria: Page entry at pages/basic-height.tsx renders in Lynx
Shows a text block rendered with native <text> element
Below it, displays pretext-computed height and lineCount
A slider or text input changes maxWidth, pretext recomputes in real time
Shows side-by-side comparison: native <text> actual height vs pretext height
Typecheck passes
Verify in Lynx DevTool that page renders correctly
userStories.notes: First demo. Uses prepare() + layout() — the simplest API. Native height can be obtained from the <text> layout event.

userStories.title: Demo — layoutWithLines manual rendering
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Lynx page that gets all lines via layoutWithLines and renders each line as an individual positioned <text> element.
acceptanceCriteria: Page entry at pages/layout-with-lines.tsx renders in Lynx
Uses prepareWithSegments + layoutWithLines to get line array
Each line rendered as an individual <text> element positioned absolutely
Shows line index, line text, and line width for each line
Slider changes maxWidth, only layoutWithLines re-runs (not prepare), lines update in real time
Typecheck passes
Verify in Lynx DevTool that lines are correctly positioned
userStories.notes: Demonstrates the rich layout API. Key point: prepare() is called once, layout is re-run on width change.

userStories.title: Demo — shrinkwrap width via walkLineRanges
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Lynx page that computes the tightest container width for multiline text using walkLineRanges.
acceptanceCriteria: Page entry at pages/shrinkwrap.tsx renders in Lynx
Shows a text block at a given maxWidth
Uses walkLineRanges to find the widest line (shrinkwrap width)
Visual comparison: container at maxWidth vs container at shrinkwrap width side by side
Pixel difference (wasted space) shown numerically
Typecheck passes
Verify in Lynx DevTool that shrinkwrap container is visually tighter
userStories.notes: Demonstrates a capability missing from native Lynx layout — multiline shrinkwrap.

userStories.title: Demo — variable-width flow via layoutNextLine
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Lynx page that flows text around a rectangular obstacle using layoutNextLine with different widths per line.
acceptanceCriteria: Page entry at pages/variable-flow.tsx renders in Lynx
Shows text flowing around a rectangular image/view obstacle
Lines beside the obstacle are narrower, lines below it are full width
Each line rendered as an individually positioned <text> element
layoutNextLine loop uses cursor continuity: each line's end becomes the next line's start
Typecheck passes
Verify in Lynx DevTool that text correctly wraps around the obstacle
userStories.notes: Demonstrates the streaming variable-width API. This is the key differentiator vs native <text> layout.

userStories.title: Demo — bubbles shrinkwrap shared logic
userStories.description: As the library, I need the bubbles-shared logic (prepareBubbleTexts, collectWrapMetrics, findTightWrapMetrics, computeBubbleRender) ported for the chat bubble demo.
acceptanceCriteria: pages/demos/bubbles-shared.ts ported from Pretext with same exports
prepareBubbleTexts(texts) calls prepareWithSegments for each text
collectWrapMetrics(prepared, maxWidth) uses walkLineRanges to find max line width and line count
findTightWrapMetrics(prepared, maxWidth) binary-searches the minimum width that preserves line count
computeBubbleRender(preparedBubbles, chatWidth) computes per-bubble CSS width, tight width, and total wasted pixels
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Source: ../pretext/pages/demos/bubbles-shared.ts. Pure logic port — no rendering. Import paths change from '../../src/layout.ts' to '../../src/layout'.

userStories.title: Demo — bubbles shrinkwrap chat page
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the chat bubble Lynx page showing per-bubble shrinkwrap with interactive width slider.
acceptanceCriteria: Page entry at pages/demos/bubbles.tsx renders in Lynx
Multiple chat message bubbles displayed with sample text
Each bubble visually shrinkwrapped to its tightest width
Slider changes chat container width, all bubbles reflow live
Shows wasted pixels comparison: CSS maxWidth vs shrinkwrap width
Adapt DOM manipulation to Lynx elements (replace document.getElementById etc.)
Typecheck passes
Verify in Lynx DevTool that bubbles shrinkwrap correctly
userStories.notes: Source: ../pretext/pages/demos/bubbles.ts + ../pretext/pages/demos/bubbles.html. Replace DOM APIs (document.getElementById, HTMLDivElement, etc.) with Lynx/ReactLynx equivalents.

userStories.title: Demo — dynamic layout wrap geometry
userStories.description: As the library, I need the wrap-geometry helpers (polygon interval, hull extraction, point-in-polygon) for the editorial layout demo.
acceptanceCriteria: pages/demos/wrap-geometry.ts ported from Pretext
getPolygonIntervalForBand() returns horizontal interval where a polygon intersects a line band
getRectIntervalsForBand() returns intervals for rect-based obstacles
carveTextLineSlots() subtracts blocked intervals from a line region
transformWrapPoints() applies rotation + translation to hull points
isPointInPolygon() tests if a point is inside a polygon (for hit testing)
getWrapHull() extracts convex hull from rasterized SVG alpha — adapt from canvas ImageData to Lynx equivalent or precompute hulls as static data
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Source: ../pretext/pages/demos/wrap-geometry.ts. The hull extraction (getWrapHull) uses OffscreenCanvas.getImageData() which Lynx likely lacks. Fallback: precompute hull points as static JSON from the browser version.

userStories.title: Demo — dynamic editorial layout page
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the editorial spread demo on Lynx: two-column text flow around rotatable logo obstacles with live reflow.
acceptanceCriteria: Page entry at pages/demos/dynamic-layout.tsx renders in Lynx
Headline text with binary-search font-size fitting (no mid-word breaks)
Body text flows left column → right column via layoutNextLine cursor continuity
Columns route around logo obstacles using wrap-geometry
Tap on logo triggers rotation animation with text reflow
Viewport resize changes column layout and triggers full reflow
DOM manipulation replaced with Lynx element APIs or ReactLynx components
Typecheck passes
Verify in Lynx DevTool that editorial layout looks correct
userStories.notes: Source: ../pretext/pages/demos/dynamic-layout.ts + ../pretext/pages/demos/dynamic-layout-text.ts + ../pretext/pages/demos/dynamic-layout.html. Most complex demo. Replace DOM APIs with Lynx equivalents. Logo SVGs from ../pretext/pages/assets/.
